problem_overview:
problem=Optimize the yield of a Baumgartner 2019 Buchwald-Hartwig C-N coupling
benchmark for the fixed nucleophile Benzamide. The search space is
restricted to the experimentally observed conditions present in the vendored
CSV. During simulation, the optimizer should not see the yield column;
yields are revealed only via the dataset oracle at the human-interrupt step.
state_reasoning_log: [warm_start] shortlist=10 target=10 pool=60 knowledge_mode=knowledge_gap strategy=Buchwald-Hartwig C-N coupling with knowledge-gap profile requires balanced exploration of catalyst/base combinations whi

steps.summary: Parsed problem specification for CC campaign.
outcome: problem=Optimize the yield of a Baumgartner 2019 Buchwald-Hartwig C-N coupling benchmark for the fixed nucleophile Benzamide. The search space is restricted to the experimentally observed conditions present in the vendored CSV. During simulation, the optimizer should not see the yield column; yields are revealed only via the dataset oracle at the human-interrupt step.

messages.content: Loaded structured problem specification from file; skipping LLM parsing.
Bootstrapped AutoBO v3 runtime: active=gp_indicator_matern52 (CoCaBO GP + CatBoost + Deep Ensemble)

reasoning: Prioritize testing H2 (bidentate ligands) and H3 (strong bases) first due to high confidence and established literature precedent for Buchwald-Hartwig couplings. Use H1 and H4 to define catalyst and temperature boundaries. Reserve H5 for later iterations if solvent effects remain unexplained.
outcome: H1 new (active, medium): Electron-rich palladium precatalysts (e.g., Pd(OAc)2, Pd2(dba)3) will outperform electron-poor alternatives due to enhanced oxidative addition to aryl halides in Buchwald-Hartwig C-N couplings.

H2 new (active, high): Bidentate phosphine ligands (e.g., Xantphos, dppf) will provide superior yields compared to monodentate alternatives due to enhanced catalyst stability and suppressed β-hydride elimination.
H3 new (active, high): Strong, non-nucleophilic bases (NaOtBu, LiHMDS) will outperform weaker bases (K2CO3, Cs2CO3) for hindered secondary amine substrates by driving deprotonation equilibrium.
H4 new (active, medium): Temperatures above 100°C will cause catalyst decomposition and reduced yields due to phosphine oxidation and Pd black formation, creating an optimal window at 80-100°C.
H5 new (active, low): Solvent polarity significantly affects yield: polar aprotic solvents (dioxane, toluene) will outperform coordinating solvents (DMF, DMSO) by preventing catalyst inhibition.

```json { "hypotheses": [ { "id": "H1", "text": "Electron-rich palladium precatalysts (e.g., Pd(OAc)2, Pd2(dba)3) will outperform electron-poor alternatives due to enhanced oxidative addition to aryl halides in Buchwald-Hartwig C-N couplings.", "mechanism": "Oxidative addition is the rate-determining step for electron-rich amines; electron-rich Pd(0) centers facilitate this step via back-donation into the C-X σ* orbital.", "testable_prediction": "Pd(OAc)2 and Pd2(dba)3 will show higher yields (>15% improvement) compared to PdCl2 or Pd(TFA)2 under identical ligand/base conditions.", "confidence": "medium", "status": "active" }, { "id": "H2", "text": "Bidentate phosphine ligands (e.g., Xantphos, dppf) will provide superior yields compared to monodentate alternatives due to enhanced catalyst stability and suppressed β-hydride elimination.", "mechanism": "Wide bite-angle bidentate ligands enforce cis geometry, accelerating reductive elimination while preventing catalyst decomposition pathways.", "testable_prediction": "Xantphos and dppf will achieve >70% yield with secondary amines where monodentate ligands (PPh3) give <50%.", "confidence": "high", "status": "active" }, [truncated]
